frontMatter.title: Kotlin 101 - Extension Function
frontMatter.summary: There is some code that we write everywhere. It doesn't have **state** and usually small enough to become a `class`. We often call it a helper function and in Java we used to save it in some `*Utils` class.

prev.title: Disable Direct Access to Your IP in Nginx
prev.summary: Often times we don't want to expose our server IP address to public. We just want to access our domain address.

next.title: Kotlin 101 - Constructors
next.summary: If you're familiar with OOP then you must be familiar with constructors. A constructors is a special method of a `class` that initialize an object of that type. Simply put, a constructors is a function that return your object.
